extension.description: The best Git features from PyCharm, VS Code, and Visual Studio IDE.
command.openInVsCodeMergeEditor: Open in VS Code Merge Editor
configuration.tooltips.enabled.markdownD: Enable or disable tooltips inside the IntelliGit window (Commit Graph, Branch Column, etc.).
configuration.clearLastCommit.markdownDe: Clear the commit message draft after a successful local commit. Disable to retain the draft in the Commit panel.
configuration.undockableWindow.markdownD: Open IntelliGit as a unified editor tab when Show Git Log is invoked, enabling native VS Code undocking to a second monitor.
configuration.undockableWindowButtonVisa: Show the Undock button in the IntelliGit graph view title. Disable to hide the button while keeping the undock command available.
configuration.worktree.includeFiles.mark: Relative gitignored files or folders to copy from the current repository into each newly created worktree, such as `.env` or `.vscode/settings.json`.
configuration.icons.enum.standard: Use VS Code's monochromatic icon style, consistent with built-in panels.
configuration.icons.markdownDescription: Icon style for the IntelliGit commit panel. `standard` uses VS Code's monochromatic icon foreground (default); `color` uses IntelliGit's colored icons.
Place the commit panel on the left side of the undocked/tabbed window.
Place the commit panel on the right side of the undocked/tabbed window.
configuration.commitWindowPosition.markd: Position of the commit panel within the undocked/tabbed IntelliGit window. `auto` (default) follows VS Code's `workbench.sideBar.location`; `left` and `right` force a side.
configuration.commitChecks.hosts.markdow: Map self-hosted Git hostnames to a commit-check provider so IntelliGit shows CI status badges for them. Keys are hostnames (no scheme or path), values are provider ids. Supported provider ids are `gitlab` and `bitbucket-server` (for self-hosted Bitbucket Data Center). Example: `{ "git.example.com": "gitlab" }`. Changes take effect after a window reload.
configuration.commitChecks.enabled.markd: Show CI/CD commit-check badges in the commit graph. When disabled, no badges are fetched or rendered for any provider. Changes take effect after a window reload.
configuration.commitChecks.providers.mar: Enable or disable commit-check badges per provider. Keys are `github`, `gitlab`, `bitbucket-cloud`, and `bitbucket-server`; set one to `false` to suppress its badges. A disabled provider that matches your origin remote yields no badge (IntelliGit does not fall through to another remote). All default to `true`. Changes take effect after a window reload.
configuration.commitChecks.ciCdFilter.ma: Override which check names count as CI/CD for the badge (GitHub and GitLab only). Provide a regular expression (matched case-insensitively against each check's name and description); leave empty to use the built-in pattern. Code-review bots are always excluded regardless of this value. Changes take effect after a window reload.
command.commitChecksSignIn: Sign In to Commit Checks Provider
command.commitChecksSignOut: Sign Out of Commit Checks Provider
